# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch-silent -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# Bench rules: compile with -DEN_PERF, run on FPGA via GDB, display cycle counts
define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

define FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
	@printf "  %-14s ref: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
	@printf "  %-14s opt: " $(1)
	$$(Q)$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

bench-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_bench.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all bench-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch-silent -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113

           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \

             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \

  MSG_CC =

  MSG_AR =

	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \

	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0

  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -DEN_PERF -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \

	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o

	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \

	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench.o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

	@$$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS) > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.txt 2>/dev/null; \

	 awk 'FNR==1{f++} /The cycle count is/{if(f==1)rc=$$5;else oc=$$5} /The inst count is/{if(f==1)ri=$$5;else oi=$$5} \
	      END{sp=(oc>0)?rc/oc:0; printf "  %-16s %8d %8d %7.2fx %8d %8d\n","$(1)",rc,oc,sp,ri,oi}' \

	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

	 $$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $$(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS) > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.txt 2>/dev/null; \
	 awk 'FNR==1{f++} /The cycle count is/{if(f==1)rc=$$5;else oc=$$5} /The inst count is/{if(f==1)ri=$$5;else oi=$$5} \
	      END{sp=(oc>0)?rc/oc:0; printf "  %-16s %8d %8d %7.2fx %8d %8d\n","$(1)",rc,oc,sp,ri,oi}' \

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \

REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

_bench_header:
	@printf "  %-16s %8s %8s %7s %8s %8s\n" "function" "ref_cyc" "opt_cyc" "speedup" "ref_ins" "opt_ins"
	@printf "  %-16s %8s %8s %7s %8s %8s\n" "--------" "-------" "-------" "-------" "-------" "-------"

#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#

#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)

  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

file.content:
---
name: FPGA benchmark compile/run approach
description: How to compile and run benchmarks on the Andes FPGA - use simple gcc with -mvh, not picolibc specs
type: feedback
---

For compiling programs to run on the Andes FPGA board:
- Just use the toolchain's built-in newlib: `riscv64-elf-gcc -Os -mvh -o foo.elf foo.c -lm`
- Do NOT use `--specs=picolibc.specs`, `--oslib=semihost`, `--crt0=semihost`, `-Wl,--defsym` hacks, etc.
- `-mvh` enables Andes virtual hosting (semihosting) — required for printf output via GDB
- Toolchain: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- GDB (no-python): `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython`
- The picolibc build (`build_mculib3.sh`) produces `libm.a` etc. for benchmarking, but linking for FPGA runs should use the simple approach above.

When benchmarking picolibc's libm specifically, link against the built picolibc libm.a directly rather than through specs.

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#   make bench-all      — build bench ELFs, run opt vs ref on FPGA via GDB
#   make bench-sin      — bench a single function on FPGA via GDB
#
# FPGA board:
#   make FPGA_REMOTE=<host:port> bench-sin   (default: sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113)
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc_zicsr -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
GDB  = $(PREFIX)gdb

FPGA_REMOTE    ?= sqa-boards.andestech.com:1113
FPGA_GDB_FLAGS  = -batch-silent -ex "target remote $(FPGA_REMOTE)" -ex "reset-and-hold" -ex "lo" -ex "c"

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  =
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%_bench.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Format `make bench-all` output to print only: `function, ref_cycle, opt_cycle, speedup(ref/opt), ref_inst, opt_inst` as a table, one line per function with a header row.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - RISC-V libm assembly optimization benchmarking on Andes FPGA board
   - GDB remote target execution via `-batch-silent -ex "target remote ..."` with semihosting (`-mvh`)
   - `nds_pfcounter.h` performance counter: `startPFM`/`stopPFM`/`readResult()` which prints "The inst count is N" and "The cycle count is N" to stdout (or stderr via GDB)
   - GNU Make `define`/`eval`/`call` macro expansion: dollar-sign escaping rules - inside a define block used with `$(eval $(call ...))`, `$$5` is required to produce `$5` in the shell (two rounds of `$`->`

 expansion)
   - awk multi-file parsing using `FNR==1{f++}` to distinguish ref vs opt output files

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/Makefile`
     - Central file modified. Contains `FUNC_RULES_BENCH_SINGLE` and `FUNC_RULES_BENCH_DOUBLE_OPT` define blocks, and `bench-all` target.
     - Added `_bench_header` target and made `bench-all` depend on it for table header.
     - Replaced raw GDB stdout print recipe with output-capture + awk parse recipe.
     - Current bench rule recipe (both SINGLE and DOUBLE_OPT variants are identical in structure):
       ```makefile
       bench-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf
       	@$(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.elf $(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS) > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.txt 2>&1; \
       	 $(GDB) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.elf $(FPGA_GDB_FLAGS) > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.txt 2>&1; \
       	 awk 'FNR==1{f++} /The cycle count is/{if(f==1)rc=$$5;else oc=$$5} /The inst count is/{if(f==1)ri=$$5;else oi=$$5} \
       	      END{sp=(oc>0)?rc/oc:0; printf "  %-16s %8d %8d %7.2fx %8d %8d\n","$(1)",rc,oc,sp,ri,oi}' \
       	      $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_bench_opt.txt
       ```
     - Current `bench-all` and header targets:
       ```makefile
       _bench_header:
       	@printf "  %-16s %8s %8s %7s %8s %8s\n" "function" "ref_cyc" "opt_cyc" "speedup" "ref_ins" "opt_ins"
       	@printf "  %-16s %8s %8s %7s %8s %8s\n" "--------" "-------" "-------" "-------" "-------" "-------"

       bench-all: _bench_header $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),bench-$(f)) \
                  $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),bench-$(f))
       	@echo "Done."
       ```
     - `.PHONY` updated to include `_bench_header`.

   - `/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/test/nds_pfcounter.h`
     - Read to understand `readResult()` output format. Confirmed it prints:
       ```
       The inst count is <N>
       The cycle count is <N>
       ```
     - `$5` is the numeric field in both lines (fields: "The", "inst/cycle", "count", "is", N).

     - Read to understand `perf()` function structure and confirm `readResult()` usage. The `perf()` function calls `startPFM`, loops 100 iterations, `stopPFM`, then `readResult()`.

), once by recipe expansion (`$5`->`$(5)`=empty Make variable). Fix: use `$$5` so the chain is `$$5` -> `$5` -> `$5` in shell.

Root cause diagnosis: GDB semihosting output likely goes to stderr, not stdout, so redirecting only stdout (`> file.txt`) left txt files empty. Fix applied: changed `2>/dev/null` to `2>&1` to capture both stdout and stderr into the txt file. This fix has NOT yet been confirmed working by the user.

content.text: The issue is clear: the normal path always saves/restores `ra` via the stack (4 instructions, 2 memory ops per call), even though only the denormal path needs `ra` for `call __clzsi2`. Removing the outer stack frame from the normal path and pushing `ra` into the denormal path's existing frame will fix it.

content.text:
The instruction count difference (1400 = 14 instructions/call * 100 calls) is exactly explained by the ref's unnecessary `s1-s4` save/restore that GCC generates conservatively. The remaining ~8% cycle gap is a code layout artifact - the two ELFs place `frexpf`, `__addsf3`, and `__floatsisf` at different addresses (opt: `frexpf` at 0x10340, ref: 0x118ec), causing different I-cache behavior. The frexpf code itself is not the bottleneck.

The fix I applied is the correct optimization - the normal path no longer spills/restores `ra` (saved 4 instructions, 2 memory ops per call), and the improvement from 0.83x to 0.92x reflects that. The remaining gap is not addressable at the function level.

If frexpf consistently benchmarks worse than ref across multiple runs, the right call is to drop it from the opt set - the code size cost isn't earning back performance.

commit 5b999a3bd73bac5c52e84a839ffbf238687d498b
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 8 21:49:38 2026 +0800

    optimize: inline CLZ in __gf_exsub and sf_fmod to eliminate call overhead
    
    Problem:
    __gf_exsub (extended-precision subtraction, called from atan/asin/acos/
    sinh/tanh paths) called __clzsi2 with a 32-byte spill of 6 registers
    (x10-x15), even though only x13/x14/x15 were live across the call.
    Similarly, fmodf had 3 __clzsi2 call sites with identical 32-byte spills:
    one on the hot path (after the main division loop) and two on subnormal
    operand paths. sf_frexp and sf_log each spilled a dead x15 register
    (immediately overwritten at the jump target) around their __clzsi2 call.
    Each call site added 12 load/store ops + call overhead per invocation.
    
    Approach:
    Replace __clzsi2 call+spill blocks with 19-instruction binary-search CLZ
    sequences using t6 and x17 as scratch (both dead at each site). Dead
    registers are dropped from spill lists entirely:
    - __gf_exsub: eliminate full 32-byte spill; x10/x11/x12 overwritten
      unconditionally at .LL27, only x13/x14/x15 needed (all preserved by CLZ)
    - sf_fmod hot path: eliminate full 32-byte spill; only x12/x14 live
      (x10/x11/x13/x15 not used after the loop exits)
    - sf_fmod cold paths (.LAspecA, .LAspecB): same, x10/x11 dead
    - sf_frexp, sf_log: remove 16-byte inner frame for dead x15
    
    Result:
    fmodf:  145641 vs 146867 cycles (ref) = 0.99x (from ~19 cycles/call saved
            on hot CLZ path; benchmark input exercises normal range)
    sinf:   283466 vs 265723 ref cycles = 1.07x (unchanged instruction count;
            __gf_exsub CLZ path not exercised by benchmark input)
    All 64 accuracy tests pass.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
